verdicts.reason: Person carrying a flower-and-offering tray on their head in a smoky street procession, an actual ritual being performed, which matches a reference article on how rituals work.
Man sitting cross-legged with eyes closed and hands in a meditation mudra among cardboard boxes; shows meditation/breathwork generically but not specifically the four-count box-breathing pattern.

Sticky notes reading 'START UP' and '1. Find investors 2. Change the world' on a laptop; touches the investor theme but shows a startup-funding tableau rather than the affirmation of personal investor identity.
Two businessmen laughing, one fanning out $100 bills with feet on the desk; gestures at wealth/money but the tone is showy money rather than the generous-presence wealth identity in the affirmation.
Woman lying peacefully on her side in a bed of white sheets, eyes closed, in soft window light — directly evokes a wind-down body scan used in bed before sleep.
A single gold coin standing on edge in moody bokeh lighting, which is the literal manifestation object the tutorial centres on (the deposit coin).
Woman in a white prayer hijab with hand on cheek looking thoughtful against a plain backdrop; no breath, sleep, or counted-breathing cue visible.
Open blank journal with a sharpened pencil resting across the page — directly shows the journal-prompt practice surface ready for free-writing.
Woman in a red dress writing in a journal on her lap outdoors; shows the journaling-practice subject the prompts feed into.
Silhouette of a person sitting cross-legged at sunset in a classic meditation pose — matches a reference article on body-based meditation.
Interior of the Mezquita-Cathedral of Córdoba with red-and-white striped Moorish arches; this is Spanish architecture, not paper marbling, and shows no marbled paper at all.
Folded printed-page paper sculptures on a black background — paper/book material in the right class but presented as art objects rather than a finished accordion-fold codex with covers.
Crumpled blank white tissue paper photographed as texture; no folded zine, no panels, no printing.
Sheet of copperplate calligraphy practice with a dip pen and pointed nib visible — directly the pressure-and-release pointed-nib subject of the tutorial.
Stack of vintage card-stock pieces and aged paper on wood; shows ephemera-style materials in the right class but no mounting onto scrapbook pages is visible.
3D-rendered cartoon lightbulb-headed robot character plugging itself into a wall outlet; nothing to do with origami or paper folding.
Streetside photo of a commercial building with a 'Tas Paper — Burnie' sign; the keyword 'paper' overlaps but the subject is a corporate building, not papermaking with botanical inclusions.
Sheet with the word 'Copperplate' rendered in flourished copperplate script on a purple ground with white blossoms — shows the named alphabet hand directly.
Pink dressmaking thread spools, a tape measure, and pink tulle; this is garment sewing with a measuring tape, not signatures sewn onto linen bookbinding tapes.
Blank white spiral sketchpad with a pencil on a dark wood plank background; no watercolour washes, blooms, or pigment present.
Black ram and a white chicken standing by a wooden shed; the right smallholding livestock class but no records, paperwork, or record-keeping system shown.
Macro of a bright red velvet mite (Trombidiidae) on a green leaf; this is a plant-dwelling mite, not Varroa destructor on bees or in a hive.
Two young lambs nuzzling in a sunny field; right species but the wrong life stage — lambs, not ewes being conditioned before tupping.
Person holding blade shears next to a sheep mid-shear with wool falling around it on a wooden barn floor — directly the hand-shearing technique named in the title.
Five hens and a rooster lined up on a perch; the right poultry class for a coop tutorial but no red mite, no coop treatment, no Dermanyssus shown.
Beekeeper in a yellow suit and veil holding a honey frame next to a row of painted hives in summer foliage — directly the summer-hive inspection subject.
Close-up of black square mesh against an orange-red clay surface, reading as a tennis-court fence or sport netting; no electric energiser, posts, or poultry visible.
Black cat curled around its small black-and-white feline kittens on a sofa; the tutorial is about rabbit kittens (a meat-rabbit doe and her young), not cats.
Beekeeper holding a frame covered in worker bees and brood; right hive-inspection class but no queen cells (the elongated peanut-shaped emergency cells) are visible.
Snowy maple-syrup sugar shack with metal sap buckets hanging on trees in late winter; this is maple sugaring, not autumn sugar-syrup feeding of a beehive.
A round mini cheesecake topped with fresh raspberries on a marble board, clearly the finished baked raspberry cheesecake.
A creamy risotto with green vegetable pieces and tomato/parsley flecks, but the green vegetable reads more as courgette than asparagus tips so the specific asparagus dish is not unambiguous.
An oval ridged flatbread that looks like Persian/Turkish barbari rather than the round, plain Trinidadian sada roti the title names.
A baked penne dish with meat sauce and melted cheese in a white oval baker, clearly a meaty pasta bake matching Sloppy Joe's Pasta Bake.
A close-up of roasted aubergine, courgette, tomato and pepper with herbs, the defining components of ratatouille.
Shows aubergine baked with tomato sauce and melted cheese (parmigiana-style), not the smoked aubergine salad with garlic and lemon named in the title.
Crispy glazed chicken pieces with visible garlic slices and sesame seeds in a sticky honey-soy glaze, matching honey garlic chicken.
Shows sliced rare beef tenderloin with rosemary on a slate, with no pastry casing or mushroom duxelles, so it is not a Wellington.
Shows raw red steak with peppercorns and rosemary, not the cooked French pepper steak with brandy cream sauce that defines Steak au Poivre.
Shows a chunky lentil and meat stew/soup with potato and carrot, not a tomato-based lentil bolognese sauce.
Shows bark mulch on the ground with a small mushroom, not a compost bin, compost heap, or vegetable scraps being composted.
Shows a rustic bowl of pale cream/sauce with thyme and a fried egg, completely unrelated to wood stove installation.
Shows three white American kerbside post-mailboxes numbered 312/314/316, not a composting toilet.
Shows a bowl of milky liquid with a spoon and herbs in a styled flat-lay, not water hardness, limescale, or an appliance affected by scale.
Shows three exterior American post-mailboxes on a stand in a garden, not a UK letterbox slot being draught-sealed with brush or cover plate.
A close-up of a domestic water meter showing the digit dials and rotating indicator, exactly the subject of the tutorial.
Shows a young girl in a hijab holding a small water bottle in a hall, no food waste, storage, or meal-planning content visible.
Shows a grid of vintage audio cassette tapes, nothing to do with secondary glazing, windows, or acrylic sheet.
Shows bowls of sorted coloured plastic bottle caps, recycling-adjacent imagery but not kerbside-bin sorting or a recycling bin in use.
Shows a styled bowl of cream/sauce with herbs on a wooden table, completely unrelated to solar panels, batteries, or inverters.
Close-up of currant-studded rock-cake style bakes, the right class of British currant bake but not the flat griddled hinnie form.
Shows cyclists at the Paris-Brest-Paris bicycle race, not the choux pastry ring with hazelnut praline cream.
A bundt-shaped cake dusted with icing sugar on a wire rack, right cake shape but no visible vanilla/chocolate marble swirl since the top is fully dusted.
A rustic round country loaf with a crisp dusty crust, matching a simple Italian house bread.
Close-up of golden scones studded with dried fruit/currants, matching sultana-studded fruit scones.
Shows a chrome supercharger with three pink butterfly valves on a car bonnet, the engine 'damper' meaning, not the Australian campfire bread.
Shows a honeybee on a beeswax honeycomb, the insect-and-comb meaning, not the brittle caramel sponge candy 'honeycomb' confection.
Dark fudgy chocolate brownies drizzled with chocolate, but only one chocolate type is clearly visible (no obvious milk or white chocolate chunks/swirl).
Shows broken sheets of nut-studded chocolate bark/bars, not a tempered chocolate band wrapped around a cake.
A plain, light, evenly-risen round sponge cake on a wire cooling rack, matching a Génoise.

Person dispensing a thin amber liquid (looks like oil) from a pump bottle onto their palm; right class (skin-care application) but reads as a body oil, not the thick oil-in-water hand-cream emulsion named.
Cracked-open coconuts on dark wood; shows a key ingredient (coconut, source of the coconut oil base) but not the deodorant paste itself.
Woman scrubbing her leg with coarse dark coffee-ground exfoliant taken from a clear jar — unambiguously a coffee body scrub in use.
Linen/muslin drawstring sachet tied with fresh lavender sprigs against a wooden background — this is a lavender sachet, exactly the named subject.
Close-up of raw honeycomb texture in a beehive; not a layered honeycomb-sheet pillar candle nor a flat foundation sheet ready for rolling.
Three pastel-coloured spherical bath bombs held in hands above a bathtub edge — clearly bath bombs, the named product.
A labelled cold-process bar (organic natural soap with coconut/olive/essential oils) styled with eucalyptus and lavender; right class but no visible oats or oatmeal texture.
An unrolled white toilet paper roll on a white background — toilet-adjacent but no fizz tabs, no bicarbonate, no toilet bowl.
Styled product shot of a goat-milk soap bar with branded honey-themed packaging, a honey jar and a bowl of milk on a wooden board — clearly goat's milk and honey soap.
A pump-top hand-soap dispenser on a bathroom sink next to a bathtub; cleaning-adjacent but it is hand soap, not a diluted castile floor cleaner, and no floor is shown.
A slot canyon (Antelope Canyon style sandstone walls) — entirely unrelated to Navajo chain-plying or any spinning technique.
A pile of dense, multi-coloured wet-felted pom-pom beads; right craft family but the opposite of cobweb felt's gauzy single-wisp sheet.
Two views of an ancient stone spindle whorl with a measuring scale — it is a spindle whorl artefact but not a top-whorl drop spindle in spinning use.
Four vintage wooden sewing-thread spools of cream, navy, white and orange thread — not the small figure-eight hand-wound butterfly bobbin used in tapestry weaving.
A manufactured straw boater hat floating in a wheat field — not a wet-felted hat and no shaping over pot/bowl/balloon.
Hands stained dark blue plunging a fabric bundle into a deep-blue vat outdoors — unambiguously indigo vat dyeing in action.
Close-up of a dressed multicoloured warp on a loom (yellow ground with pink, blue and dark stripes) — the loom is threaded and beamed, exactly the named subject.
A manufactured black leather belt with a brass buckle on jeans — a belt but not macrame; no square knots or cotton cord visible.
Gloved hand squeezing purple dye onto a rainbow tie-dyed fabric bundle — a different fibre-arts technique entirely; no drum carder, no fibre batt.
Identical pile of wet-felted pom-pom beads as image 71 — round felted balls, no needle, no wire armature, no poseable figure.
Close-up of a euro-cylinder lock barrel with its key inserted, branded HOPPE — this is the euro-cylinder component named in the title.
Macro photo of a house fly on a green leaf — entirely unrelated to PTFE tape, jointing compound or BSP fittings.
Two men sawing through a length of bamboo with a handsaw in a street — wood-cutting adjacent but not the marking, sawing of shoulders and chiselling out of a dado that defines a housing joint.
Dramatic black-and-white close-up of clay-coated or oil-coated hands cradling something — no consumer unit, no MCBs, no electrical hardware visible.
A flat wood-plank background texture with no person, no sander and no sanding action visible — wood-adjacent but not floor sanding.
Wide shot of a wall part-coated with a thin grey-white plaster skim showing trowel strokes and partial coverage — clearly a skim coat in progress.
A closed white panelled front door dressed with a Christmas wreath, jingle bells and a wooden welcome sign — door shown decoratively but no fitting, planing or hinging.
A massive Icelandic waterfall (Skogafoss-style) with a tiny figure on a black-gravel beach — entirely unrelated to mould treatment or wall painting.

Beige tile adhesive combed in ridges with a notched trowel beside laid tiles and blue levelling clips — tile-laying adjacent but the wrong stage; this is bedding mortar, not grout being pressed into the joints.
Ornate antique brass door knob and escutcheon on a polished mahogany door — door hardware, not a ceiling rose nor any lighting circuit work.
Image shows a finished carved birch kuksa with the characteristic hooked handle and hollowed bowl, exactly the named subject.
Image shows a pair of glasses resting on a dark wooden board; wood is visible but grain direction is not being read or demonstrated.
Image shows pasta with a cherry tomato and basil on a metal spoon — a food shot, not a carved cherry wooden serving spoon.
Image shows hands setting a plate on a woven placemat; the mat looks like patterned fabric/woven cloth rather than a twisted-bulrush chequerboard rush mat.
Image shows a studio scene with a bar stool draped in fake grapes and a small still-life — a finished mass-produced stool, not a green-wood drawknifed stool leg.
Image shows a wooden honey dipper with characteristic turned rings dripping honey into a pot, exactly the named subject.
Image shows sliced bread on a slatted wooden serving board; it is a bread-serving board but flat-slatted, not the carved beech round with raised rim described.
Image shows a light wooden picture frame with a vase of oak leaves beside it; a frame is present but no mortise and tenon joinery is visible.
Image shows a woman's legs on a wooden beach chair beside a sandal on sand — a holiday scene unrelated to a wood-carving stop cut.
Image shows a cut log end-grain with a single radial split — a riven-like split in oak-coloured wood, but it is a tree cross-section, not a shaped chair leg.
Image shows a plate piled with fresh chamomile flowers — the named botanical alone, with no compress or eye preparation visible.
Image shows commercial ginger-beer gift packs with bottles and raw ginger root on a display table — a product shot, not a homemade ginger infusion.
Image shows whole garlic bulbs from above with no honey or maceration jar visible — one named ingredient alone.
Image shows a glass jar of red infusion with a mesh tea ball steeping inside and herbs/flowers beside it — the extraction process the tutorial explains.
Image shows calendula flowers growing in a garden — the named botanical alone, with no compress or wound-care preparation visible.
Image shows a lavender field at sunset — the named botanical alone, with no compress or bite-care preparation visible.
Image shows a jar of dried herbs/seeds with sprig and nuts on linen — a generic herbal-preparation flat lay rather than anything depicting red-flag/safety warnings.
Image shows fresh stinging nettle plants growing in a patch — the named botanical alone, with no infusion or cup visible.
Image is a close-up of an echinacea flower with no tincture jar or alcohol-extraction vessel visible — the exact example called PARTIAL in the rubric.
Image shows a hanging cluster of black elderberries on red stems with elderberry foliage — clearly identifiable Sambucus nigra, matching the profile subject.
Image shows a fired ceramic bowl filled with bean sprouts and sauce — a food dish, not a slab-built jewellery dish in air-dry clay.
Image shows hands forming raw clay coils on a workbench — the coil-building technique is visible, but no planter form has been built yet.
Image shows a wrapped block of clay with brushes, palette knives and a slip dish — pottery materials laid out flat, but no flat slab wall hanging is shown.
Image shows a vase being thrown on a potter's wheel with a rib tool — wheel-thrown construction is the opposite of the named slab-built panel assembly.
Image shows a workshop bench of small wheel-thrown jugs and pots in raw clay — pottery vessels rather than a flat paper-clay name plaque.
Image shows the same bench of small wheel-thrown jugs and pots in raw clay — none of it is a flat clay slab being rolled out.
Image shows a turned dark-wood/burl ball-shaped vessel holding a tealight — wooden, not pinched clay, and with no pierced light-projecting holes.
Image shows a ceramic street-name plaque (Triq San Duminku) mounted on stone — a ceramic name sign, but the lettering is painted rather than incised into clay.
Image shows weathered curved terracotta roof tiles stacked on a roof — fired-clay tiles, but not flat leaf-impression air-dry clay tiles.
Image shows a lump of clay centred on a potter's wheel with a slip bucket beside it — a wheel-throwing setup, not slip being trailed from a squeeze bottle onto clay.
Shows chocolate-topped round biscuits on a plate, the right class, but afghans specifically have a cornflake texture and walnut garnish not visible here.
Shows croissants topped with flaked almonds on paper, unambiguously matching almond croissants re-baked with almond cream.
Shows small round cakes dusted with icing sugar but financiers are rectangular or boat-shaped; the shape and sage prop suggest a different small cake.
Shows round white crinkled biscuits in a paper-lined basket, exactly matching the appearance of soft amaretti.
Shows a white-frosted cake from above, the right class for a buttercream tutorial, but the herb garnish is inconsistent with a crusting buttercream demonstration.
Shows a tall tube-pan sponge with white icing and a slice removed, which is the characteristic form of angel food cake.
Shows round golden biscuits with rolled oats on top on a plate, matching the appearance of Anzac biscuits.
Shows a slice of lattice-topped pie with clearly visible dark blackberry and apple filling served with cream, matching apple and blackberry pie.
Shows an apple crumble pie with a streusel top, not a German sunken apple cake which has fanned apple slices pressed into a butter cake batter.
Shows an apple pie with a crumble and streusel top and apples beside it, matching apple crumble pie.
Shows two raw apples on a red background; apple fritters are battered deep-fried apple rings, a completely different subject.
Shows an apple pie with a crumble and streusel top, matching Dutch apple pie which is specifically defined by its crumble lid instead of a pastry top.
Shows a slice of flaky layered pastry roll with apple filling dusted with icing sugar and served with cream, clearly apple strudel.
Shows square pastry pockets with crimped edges, sugar glaze, and apples in the background, matching apple turnovers made from rough puff pastry.
Shows a twisted braided loaf with visible green herb filling woven through the dough on newspaper, matching savoury babka with cheese and herbs.
Shows individual pastry cases with white icing and a glace cherry; the classic Bakewell tart is a full-size tart with exposed frangipane, not iced individual tarts.
Shows fresh cherries in a bowl with baking tools and a small tart in the corner; the cherry context is relevant but the finished Cherry Bakewell tart is not the main subject.
Shows a rectangular piece of baklava with crushed nuts and syrup on a pink plate, clearly and unambiguously showing baklava.
Shows a golden-brown banana bread loaf in a tin on an oven rack, unambiguously showing the finished banana bread.
Shows a sliced banana bread loaf with icing drizzle and banana chip garnish, clearly showing banana bread.
Shows banana bread with icing drizzle and banana chip garnish but no walnuts are visible; the topping style suggests a different variant.
Shows fried banana pieces on a banana leaf, adjacent to the subject but banana fritters are specifically battered and the pieces here lack a batter coating.
Shows Danish-style square puff pastries with cream and fresh berry toppings, not oval spiced currant pastry Banbury cakes.
Shows a smooth cream-coloured cheesecake with a slice removed, but banoffee cheesecake specifically requires visible banana and dulce de leche elements which are absent.
Shows a flat round bread cut into quarters with rosemary garnish, not bara brith which is a dark dense Welsh fruit loaf studded with dried fruit.
Shows raw white granulated sugar in a wooden bowl; barley sugar is an amber boiled sweet twisted into sticks, a completely different finished product.
Shows flat white disc-shaped rounds on a plate that look like pikelets or flatbreads, not barmbrack which is an enriched yeasted fruit loaf dark with sultanas and mixed spice.
Shows round golden yeasted buns with sugar crystals and herb garnish; close to Bath buns but the herb decoration and absence of visible currants make it ambiguous.
